steps.title: Backsolving by testing the candidate
steps.content:
Backsolving by testing the candidate (C(d)=72d-36):
For $d=1$, expected cost is 72 dollars.
$$C(1)=72\cdot1-36=36$$
Since $36\neq72$, the remaining alternative fails.

steps.title: Backsolving by testing the candidate
steps.content:
Backsolving by testing the candidate (C(d)=36d+36):
For $d=1$, expected cost is 72 dollars.
$$C(1)=36\cdot1+36=72$$
For $d=2$, expected cost is 72 dollars plus 36 dollars, or 108 dollars.
$$C(2)=36\cdot2+36=108$$
That matches the correct answer — A is correct.
